name: Water-surface vortices drive tiny rotors without electricity, magnets or chemica
what: Water-surface vortices offer a new, efficient method for generating controlled miniature rotations.

name: Quantum internet leaves the lab with first real-world entanglement over busy tel
what: Quantum internet's real-world demonstration could enhance secure communication networks.

name: Dyson Swarm Milestone Roadmap
what: The Dyson Swarm is a hypothetical megastructure consisting of satellites and solar panels orbiting the Earth to capture solar energy. This roadmap outlines key milestones for developing and deploying such a system.

name: Simulation Theory Implications for Technology
what: The simulation theory suggests that our reality is a computer-generated simulation. This concept has profound implications for technology, including the development of advanced AI and virtual environments.
source: University of Oxford · University of Washington

name: Digital Physics
what: Digital physics explores the idea that the universe can be described as a giant computer, with physical laws and phenomena arising from computational processes.
source: University of California, Santa Cruz · Perimeter Institute

name: 4D-Printing Materials
what: 4D-printing materials can change their shape or properties over time in response to external factors like temperature, humidity, and light.

name: Programmable Matter
what: Programmable matter refers to materials that can change their shape, structure, or properties in response to external stimuli.

name: Silicene (2D Material)
what: Silicene is a two-dimensional material derived from silicon, with potential applications in electronics and optoelectronics due to its unique electronic properties.

name: Borophene (2D Material)
what: Borophene is a two-dimensional material with exceptional electrical and thermal conductivity.
source: University of Texas at Dallas · Rice University

name: Topological Insulators
what: Topological insulators are materials that conduct electricity on their surface while being electrically insulating in the bulk.

name: MAX Phase Ceramics
what: MAX phase ceramics are a family of materials with unique properties, including high thermal stability and electrical conductivity.
source: Stanford University · Lawrence Livermore National Lab

name: Green Ammonia Production
what: Green ammonia is produced using renewable energy sources to power the Haber-Bosch process, reducing greenhouse gas emissions and providing a sustainable alternative to traditional fossil fuel-based ammonia production.

name: Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS)
what: Enhanced Geothermal Systems involve drilling deeper and using advanced techniques to access geothermal energy from hotter, more distant regions.

name: Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion (OTEC)
what: Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion harnesses the temperature difference between warm surface water and cold deep ocean water to generate electricity.

name: CAR-T 2.0
what: Next-generation CAR-T therapies are designed to improve the specificity and efficacy of T-cell immunotherapies, reducing side effects and enhancing long-term patient outcomes.

name: Organ-on-Chip Technology
what: Organ-on-chip technology involves creating miniature, functional models of human organs that can be used for drug testing and disease modeling.

name: Precision Oncology (Liquid Biopsies)
what: Precision oncology uses liquid biopsies to detect circulating tumor cells and DNA fragments in the bloodstream, enabling early detection and personalized treatment plans.

name: RAG Pipelines
what: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines combine the strengths of retrieval-based and generative models to improve the accuracy and relevance of generated content.

name: On-Device AI
what: On-device AI refers to the development of machine learning models that can operate on resource-constrained devices, such as smartphones and IoT sensors.

name: Radiation-resistant materials
what: Materials designed to withstand high levels of radiation exposure.

name: Aerogel Insulation
what: Advanced aerogel materials for thermal insulation in various applications.

name: CNT Tethers
what: Carbon nanotube-based space tethers for satellite deployment and maintenance.

name: Policy Generation
what: AI systems for generating policy documents and proposals.

name: Compliance Automation
what: AI tools for automating compliance processes in various industries.
source: ACM Transactions on Management Information Systems

name: Regulatory AI
what: AI systems for automating regulatory processes and drafting legislation.
source: Journal of Law, Information & Computer Science

name: Battery Chemistry AI
what: AI-driven approaches for developing new battery chemistries.

name: Fusion Plasma Control AI
what: AI systems for controlling and optimizing fusion plasma conditions.

name: Grid Optimisation AI
what: AI algorithms for optimizing energy grid operations and management.

name: Digital Physics and Simulation Theory
what: The convergence of physics, computer science, and mathematics will lead to the development of digital physics, where the universe is modeled as a vast computational system.

name: Synthetic Blood Production
what: The development of synthetic blood will revolutionize the medical field by providing a safe, unlimited supply of blood for transfusions and reducing the risk of disease transmission.

name: Neuromorphic Computing
what: Neuromorphic computing mimics the structure and function of biological neural networks to create highly efficient, low-power AI systems capable of real-time processing.

name: Quantum Neural Networks
what: Quantum neural networks leverage quantum computing to process and learn from data at an exponential speed, enabling breakthroughs in AI that are currently beyond classical limits.

name: Solid-State Batteries
what: Next-generation batteries with solid electrolytes.

name: Perovskite Solar
what: High-efficiency solar cells made from perovskites.

name: mRNA Platforms
what: A technology that uses mRNA to deliver therapeutic or prophylactic treatments.

name: CAR-T Cell Engineering
what: A type of immunotherapy that uses genetically modified T-cells to fight cancer.

name: Gene Therapy
what: A medical treatment that uses genetic material to treat or prevent diseases.

name: Protein Design AI (AlphaFold2)
what: An advanced AI tool for protein design that builds on AlphaFold's capabilities.

name: Generative Materials Design
what: A technology that uses AI to design new materials with specific properties.

name: Regulatory AI (AI for Policy and Law)
what: AI is used to analyze policy proposals, predict legal outcomes, and support regulatory decision-making.

name: Compliance Automation (AI for Compliance Management)
what: AI is used to automate compliance processes and ensure regulatory adherence.

name: Policy Generation (AI for Policy Creation)
what: AI is used to generate and optimize policy proposals.

name: Gene Therapy (AI for Genetic Diseases)
what: AI is used to design and deliver gene therapies for genetic diseases.

name: CAR-T Cell Engineering (AI for Cancer Treatment)
what: AI is used to engineer CAR-T cells for more effective cancer treatment.

name: mRNA Platforms (AI for Vaccine Development)
what: AI is used to design and optimize mRNA platforms for vaccine development.

name: Perovskite Solar (Materials for Solar Energy)
what: Perovskites are used in solar cells to increase efficiency and reduce costs.

name: Solid-State Batteries (Energy Storage Solutions)
what: Solid-state batteries use solid electrolytes to improve safety and energy density.

name: Radiation-Resistant Materials (Materials for Space)
what: Special materials designed to withstand the harsh radiation environment in space.
